body.title: Build /map-igor skill — Haiku, on-demand JSON snapshot of Igor
body.description:
Haiku-level skill that produces an on-demand inspection snapshot of Igor's full state. Output: ~/.TheIgors/maps/igor-map-<YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS>.json (greppable, parseable, diffable) plus a one-screen stdout summary.

Sections: palace tree (paths, titles, types, child counts); rules (full content); subsystem index (load-bearing area → primary file); tickets (open by status with size + inertia tags); slates (today + last 3 days); decisions (last 30 days, one-line each); gates (IGOR_TIER5_ENABLED, IGOR_ARBITER_ENABLED, others, current state); MCP servers + tools; IMAP buses + mailboxes + retention; channels (config + last 24h activity); inbox (unread by kind/urgency); logs (paths, sizes, rotation, last-modified); startup reads (every file Igor reads at boot — .env, configs, with paths and last-modified); DB schema (tables, row counts, recent write activity); runtime (~/.TheIgors/ tree sizes); code map (wild_igor/ top-level dirs + primary file per area); processes (Igor running where, tmux sessions, Ollama instances per machine).

Diff mode: /map-igor --since=yesterday produces only the delta against the prior map. End-of-day-friendly.

Stand-alone (not in audit family) but readable by audit-day and audit-audits as one of their inputs.

**Affected files:** new ~/.claude/skills/map-igor/SKILL.md; new lab/claudecode/map_igor.py with the snapshot logic.
**Design rules:** no persistence (snapshots in runtime dir, not DB). oop-first: IgorMap class with section_collectors dict. docs-in-code: top-of-file docstring on map_igor.py listing every section and where its data comes from.
**Test plan:** /map-igor produces a non-empty JSON file with all sections. Each section has a known data source verified end-to-end. /map-igor --since=yesterday computes a structural diff. Snapshot file size is reasonable (<1MB typical, <10MB worst-case).
